title: hieracium aymericianum subsp. lagraveanum
intro: Hieracium aymericianum subsp. lagraveanum, also known as Lagrange's hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium aymericianum plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium aymericianum subsp. lagraveanum include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Hierbal preparations of the plant often involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
